{"kind":"molecule","gloss":"a state when water comes down from the {urn:molecule-v0:sky|sky} [m] at many times.","groundingNote":"a state when water comes down from the {urn:molecule-v0:sky|sky} [m] at many times.","groundingRefs":["urn:molecule-v0:sky"]}

<<<KOT:reductionism>>>>
{"kind":"cannot-formalise","why":"reductionism is a philosophical position about explanation and metaphysics—how one should understand complex systems by decomposing them into parts. Its identity rests on normative claims about methodology and ontological commitments (holism vs. atomism, emergence, materialism) that exceed the prime lexicon. The catalog primes do not express philosophical stances, explanatory frameworks, or the meta-level reasoning that distinguishes reductionism from simple part-decomposition. This is a doctrine concept, not a perceptible thing or action."}
<<<END:reductionism>>>>

<<<KOT:reticulocyte>>>>
{"kind":"cannot-formalise","why":"a reticulocyte is a hematology-specific cell type defined by its developmental stage (post-marrow, pre-mature erythrocyte), ultrastructural features (ribosomal RNA mesh network), and staining properties (new methylene blue reactivity). Its identity conditions depend on biomolecular and histological criteria—cell nucleus absence, RNA content, bone-marrow origin, circulation timeline—that are not grounded in the prime lexicon and would require technical biology catalog concepts not available here."}
<<<END:reticulocyte>>>>

<<<KOT:romanticistic>>>>
{"kind":"cannot-formalise","why":"romanticistic is a literary and philosophical adjective denoting allegiance to or resemblance with Romanticism, a historical cultural and artistic movement. Its meaning is entirely parasitic on the proper-noun doctrine (Romanticism as an epoch, aesthetic ideology, set of values), and has no stable perceptual or behavioural anchoring independent of that movement. The prime lexicon cannot express historical periods, aesthetic schools, or doctrinal positions."}
<<<END:romanticistic>>>>
